Five Days · Rajasthan, India

Bundi,
Rajasthan

A crumbling fort above a blue city that Kipling wrote about and Instagram hasn't found yet — rent a haveli, own the place, and feel like you're somewhere genuinely rare.

The Vibe

Bundi is what Jaipur and Udaipur used to be before everyone arrived. A small city of blue-washed houses climbing a hillside, overlooked by a crumbling palace and a fort that requires actual effort to reach. Stepwells around every corner. Monkeys on the rooftops. It has the feeling of a place that hasn't quite been discovered yet — even though it has.

Where You'll Stay

A full haveli takeover — a historic mansion in the old city, rented exclusively for the group. High ceilings, painted walls, a rooftop terrace with fort views, and the kind of atmosphere that no hotel can manufacture. This is where Bundi beats every other option: the accommodation is part of the story.

Five Days in Bundi

Day One · Sat 5 Dec — Arrival

First Light on the Fort

Fly Bangkok to Jaipur, drive three and a half hours through open Rajasthan countryside to Bundi — arriving late afternoon as the light goes gold. Check into the haveli, claim your rooms, get your bearings on the rooftop terrace. The fort looms above you, lit up after dark. Dinner together on the roof — the first of many.

Day Two · Sun 6 Dec

The Fort and the Palace

Start at Taragarh Fort — built in 1354, perched 500 metres above the city, largely unrestored and all the better for it. Walk the ramparts, find the hidden stepwells in the undergrowth, look down at the entire city spread below. Descend to Garh Palace in the afternoon — the murals in the Chitrashala are among the finest Rajput paintings surviving anywhere. Evening: wander the old city bazaar as the day cools down.

Day Three · Mon 7 Dec

Stepwells and the River

Morning stepwell walking tour with a local guide — Raniji ki Baori, 150 feet deep and carved from floor to ceiling, plus the smaller baoris hidden through the old city lanes. After lunch, Chambal River safari: dramatic gorges, extraordinary birdlife, and gharial crocodiles emerging onto sandbars — one of the stranger wildlife encounters you can have in India.

Day Four · Tue 8 Dec

Rock Paintings and Open Country

Morning drive to Garardha — prehistoric rock paintings thousands of years old, set in open scrub country that feels genuinely remote. Back through Ramgarh Vishdhari Tiger Reserve for an afternoon wildlife drive. End at Nawal Sagar Lake at golden hour: the half-submerged temple of Varuna sitting in the middle of the water, the city reflected all around it. Final haveli dinner.

Day Five · Wed 9 Dec — Free Day

Slow Bundi

The day belongs to no itinerary. Wander the old city at your own pace, browse the silver jewellery shops, watch a local miniature painting artist at work, or simply sit on the rooftop with a chai and look at the fort. Those who want one last adventure can ride out to Talwas Fort or the Phool Sagar Lake. Back together for a long last dinner.

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Jaipur → Chennai

Easy morning in Bundi — last chai on the rooftop. Drive back to Jaipur (3.5 hours). Fly Jaipur to Chennai (approximately 2 hours). Arrive Chennai by early evening, in time for the wedding festivities to begin.

Also Worth Knowing

Getting There

Fly Bangkok → Jaipur, approximately 2.5 hours. Jaipur to Bundi is a 3.5 hour drive through open Rajasthan countryside — factor this into Day 1 timing.

Joining the Wedding

Drive back to Jaipur on Day 6 (3.5 hrs), then fly Jaipur → Chennai (~2 hrs). Arrive Chennai by early evening on 10 December.

When to Go

December is peak Rajasthan season — warm days, cool nights, skies clear enough to see the fort from across the city. The Bundi Utsav festival sometimes falls in this window — worth checking.

What to Eat

Dal baati churma is the Rajasthani staple. Bundi's local sweet is bundi — the ladoo is named after the city. Try it from a street vendor, not a hotel.

Accommodation Tip

Book a whole haveli rather than individual rooms. Ishwari Niwas and Haveli Braj Bhanushree both offer full-property rental for groups — book early, December fills fast.

Chambal Safari

Book in advance — the better operators run private boats and know the crocodile sandbars. Best in the early morning or late afternoon light.

Everyone goes to Jaipur. The people who've been to Bundi don't tell you about it — they just go back.

Bundi · Rajasthan · India
